stories.title: Ticket dashboard: local static page rendering prd.json stories
stories.description: Build a zero-build local dashboard so a human can view the ticket backlog and live story state in a browser instead of a Notion board. Static HTML + vanilla JS/CSS only, living in a new dashboard/ directory. The page fetches ../prd.json and renders one card per story, grouped into columns by status. It is served with a plain static file server from the repo root (browsers block fetch over file://), e.g. `uv run python -m http.server 8000`, then opened at http://localhost:8000/dashboard/. No frameworks, no build step, no new Python dependencies.
acceptance_criteria: dashboard/index.html exists and contains all markup, styles, and script (or links only to sibling files inside dashboard/); no framework, bundler, or CDN dependency
When served from the repo root with a static file server, the page fetches prd.json and renders one card per story showing id, type, title, priority, and acceptance criteria
Cards are grouped into four columns by status: open, in_progress, blocked, done; a story with passes=true is visually marked as passing
If prd.json cannot be fetched or parsed, the page shows a readable error message instead of a blank screen
A comment at the top of dashboard/index.html documents the serve command and URL
A pytest test (tests/test_dashboard.py) asserts dashboard/index.html exists and references prd.json, and the full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: Add 'you are headless' rule to all stage command prompts
stories.description: In test runs 3-4, stage agents that hit contradictory constraints ended their turn by asking the nonexistent human a question ('Two ways forward, your call') instead of reporting blocked in the status block; run 3's planner lost its entire plan text that way because only the final message survives a stage. Add an explicit rule to every stage command file (commands/PLAN.md, IMPLEMENT.md, TEST.md, REVIEW.md, and PRIME.md if it gives behavioral instructions): you are running headless, nobody will ever answer a question, and any contradiction or blocker must be reported as outcome=blocked in the structured status block with the reason in failure_reason. Evidence: observability/test_run1.md follow-up 2.
acceptance_criteria: Each of commands/PLAN.md, commands/IMPLEMENT.md, commands/TEST.md, commands/REVIEW.md contains an explicit headless rule: no human will answer questions, and contradictions/blockers must be reported as outcome=blocked in the status block instead of asking
The rule names the status block as the only channel for blockers (failure_reason carries the why)
A pytest test asserts the headless rule text is present in all four stage command files so it cannot silently regress
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: Circuit breaker: trip immediately on repeated dead-on-arrival stages
stories.description: Test run 2 burned all iterations re-trying identical instant failures: the CLI exited non-zero with 0 tokens used and empty output, three times in a row, and the breaker never fired because the same_error_loops threshold (5) can never be reached before max_iterations (5) under default budgets. Add an instant-failure rule to adw/safety.py CircuitBreaker: a stage result with exit_code != 0 AND tokens_used == 0 AND empty raw output counts as dead-on-arrival; after 2 consecutive such results, open the circuit regardless of other thresholds. Make the threshold a budgets.json knob (instant_failure_cap, default 2) wired through SafetyConfig.from_budgets like permission_denial_cap. Evidence: observability/test_run1.md follow-up 1 and the run 2 row of its table.
acceptance_criteria: CircuitBreaker.record opens the circuit (returns a halt reason naming instant failure) after 2 consecutive dead-on-arrival results: exit_code != 0, tokens_used == 0, empty raw output
A successful or token-consuming stage result resets the consecutive counter
The threshold is read from configs/budgets.json key instant_failure_cap (default 2) via SafetyConfig.from_budgets
Unit tests cover: trip at 2 consecutive instant failures, reset on a live result, and config override of the cap
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: Bug + trivial workflows so bug/chore tickets become runnable
stories.description: architecture.md names bug_plan_implement_test.py and trivial_implement_test.py but only feat_full_cycle.py exists, so ticket types bug and chore are schema-valid but unrunnable (audit finding A3, plans/improvements.md). Compose the two workflows from existing adw/ pieces: bug runs plan -> implement -> test (no review stage; the test stage's regression evidence is the gate), trivial runs implement -> test for chore-sized changes. Factor shared setup (branch, prompt composition, invoke wiring) out of feat_full_cycle.py rather than copying it.
acceptance_criteria: workflows/bug_plan_implement_test.py runs plan, implement, test stages for a ticket and honors the same breaker, budgets, and state bookkeeping as feat_full_cycle
workflows/trivial_implement_test.py runs implement, test stages with the same guarantees
Shared orchestration setup is factored so the three workflows do not triplicate prompt-composition or git bookkeeping code
Stage order remains owned by the workflow scripts, never by prompts
Unit tests cover stage sequencing of both new workflows with a fake invoke_fn
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: Backlog runner: outer loop over open stories
stories.description: v1 runs one ticket per human invocation. Add workflows/run_backlog.py (improvements C6, snarktank/ralph pattern): a thin outer loop that, while open stories with passes=false remain, invokes the per-ticket workflow for the next story, honoring the circuit-breaker cooldown gate between tickets and stopping on the first blocked/halted outcome instead of skipping it. The inner safety model is untouched; the runner adds no new authority.
acceptance_criteria: workflows/run_backlog.py loops: pick next open story, run its workflow, repeat until no open stories remain
An active cooldown in state.json pauses or stops the loop; it is never silently overridden
A blocked or halted ticket stops the loop with a clear printed reason (no skipping to the next story)
A --max-tickets N flag bounds the loop (bounded everything, architecture.md principle 5)
Unit tests cover loop termination on: backlog empty, blocked outcome, cooldown, and max-tickets
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: GitHub Issues intake + done/blocked notifications (phone-facing backlog)
stories.description: Bridge to the remote-operation goal: tickets arranged from a phone, outcomes reported back to it. Intake: a sync script pulls GitHub Issues labeled adw from the repo into prd.json stories (issue body carries description + acceptance criteria; labels carry type/priority), so the GitHub mobile app becomes the write-capable taskboard. Outbound: when a workflow finishes a ticket, it opens/updates a PR for the adw/<id> branch and comments the outcome (done/blocked + reason) on the source issue, so GitHub push notifications reach the phone. Requires the existing git-credential token; no new services.
acceptance_criteria: workflows/sync_issues.py converts open GitHub Issues labeled adw into valid prd.json stories with a deterministic id mapping and never duplicates existing stories
Malformed issues are skipped with a printed reason, never written into prd.json
On ticket completion the workflow opens (or updates) a PR for adw/<id> and comments done/blocked + reason on the source issue
All GitHub calls go through the REST API with the locally stored credential; the scripts degrade with a clear error when offline
Unit tests cover the issue-to-story mapping and skip logic with fixture payloads (no live API in tests)
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: Container isolation for unattended runs
stories.description: The loop currently runs on the bare Win11 host - the one open violation of the safety floor (improvements C1, highest-leverage safety item; adopt the textcortex/claude-code-sandbox Docker pattern). Run ADW_TICKET_RUN stage sessions inside a container with the repo mounted, no real credentials beyond a scoped git token, and network restricted to what stages need. This is the precondition for genuinely unattended operation and for any future --skip-permissions mode.
acceptance_criteria: Stage invocations run inside a container when an isolation flag is set; the host filesystem outside the repo mount is unreachable
No host credentials are visible inside the container beyond the scoped git push token
Hooks (PreToolUse guard, Stop checklist, autocommit) still function inside the container
A documented fallback runs stages on the host exactly as today when isolation is off
README documents the isolation setup and its limits
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green

stories.title: Document stage: per-ticket change doc for the merge-gate human
stories.description: Add a fifth, post-gate 'document' stage so the merge-gate human reviews an organized technical delta (endpoints, schemas, components, pages, CLI surfaces) instead of raw diff + run logs. It runs exactly once per ticket, after the review stage passes the dual gate (outcome=success AND exit_signal=true) and before the workflow reports done - never inside the bounded plan->implement->test->review loop, and it can never send the ticket back to plan. The stage reads the ticket, git diff main...HEAD, and the run's prior stage outputs, then writes and commits docs/changes/<ticket-id>.md (kept forever; one file per ticket; docs/ is deliberately not a harness directory). Registration: add 'document' to STAGES in adw/state.py (adw/status.py validates stage names against it) and to STAGE_TOOLS in adw/invoke.py with scope Read, Glob, Grep, Write, the existing _GIT_READONLY rules, plus Bash(git add:*) and Bash(git commit:*); add "document": "sonnet" to configs/models.json. Prompt assets: commands/DOCUMENT.md (entry prompt; must state that the agent runs headless - nobody will answer questions, blockers go in the status block - and that the doc must be committed before stopping, since the Stop checklist's clean-tree rule applies to this writing stage) and stage_specs/document_feat.md (the contract). Doc template sections, each included only when that change-kind exists in the diff: What shipped; Surface changes with subsections Endpoints/APIs, Schemas & data, Components/pages, CLI/workflows; Behavior & breaking changes; How it was verified (acceptance criteria mapped to test evidence); Review notes; File map (changed files, one line of why each). Contract rules: every claim anchored in the actual diff (never the plan's intentions), organized delta not a diff dump, bounded length, no future-work speculation, README/architecture drift is noted in the doc as a suggested follow-up and never edited. Orchestrator: in adw/orchestrator.py run_ticket, after the dual gate passes, invoke the document stage once with exactly one retry on failure/timeout; persistent failure still returns done with a warning recorded on the TicketOutcome, and a breaker halt reason from the document stage downgrades to a warning rather than converting done into halted. No hook changes are needed.
acceptance_criteria: 'document' is registered in STAGES (adw/state.py) and STAGE_TOOLS (adw/invoke.py) with exactly Read, Glob, Grep, Write, the read-only git rules, Bash(git add:*), Bash(git commit:*); configs/models.json maps document to sonnet

commands/DOCUMENT.md and stage_specs/document_feat.md exist with the repo's standard front-matter (name, description, read_when, sdlc_stage), define the conditional section template (What shipped; Endpoints/APIs; Schemas & data; Components/pages; CLI/workflows; Behavior & breaking changes; How it was verified; Review notes; File map) and the anchored-in-diff / no-diff-dump / bounded-length / no-speculation rules; DOCUMENT.md states the headless rule and the commit-before-stop requirement
adw/orchestrator.py run_ticket invokes the document stage exactly once, only after review reports outcome=success with exit_signal=true, and never on iterations that loop back to plan

Document-stage failure or timeout is retried exactly once; persistent failure still returns outcome 'done' with the warning recorded on TicketOutcome; a breaker halt reason raised during the document stage becomes a warning, never a halted outcome

Unit tests with a fake invoke_fn cover: document runs once after the dual gate; document is not invoked when review fails or loops; document failure plus one retry still yields done with the warning recorded
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green
